text.title: Usability Testing Examples and Agentic Workflow
text.summary: Good and bad task examples, severity example, and agentic workflow with prompt patterns and gotchas.

example.note: Scenario: You need to change your delivery address for an upcoming order. Task: Find your recent order and update the shipping address. Success: User locates order and changes address without facilitator help.
"Click the account button and select orders" — gives away the solution path, invalidating the observation of whether users can discover it themselves.
"Find the settings" — too vague. No success criterion. Different observers will disagree on success.

Finding: Checkout "Place Order" button not visible without scrolling on mobile. Severity: Critical (7 of 8 participants failed to complete checkout without help). Task: Purchase a product. Frequency: 7/8 participants. Problem: Button below fold on 375px viewport. Evidence: P1 "I don't know how to finish", P3 tapped Back three times. Recommendation: Fix button position above fold on mobile, or add sticky CTA bar.

antipattern.description: Helping participants who are struggling during tasks.
antipattern.reason: Every moment of struggle is data. Helping invalidates the observation and corrupts the participant's remaining session data.

antipattern.description: Recruiting 5 total participants across 3 user segments (fewer than 2 per segment).
antipattern.reason: Pattern detection requires 5 participants per segment, not 5 total. Cross-segment findings are meaningless without sufficient N per segment.

antipattern.description: Reporting only qualitative observations without severity ratings or frequency counts.
antipattern.reason: Unrated findings give engineering no basis for prioritization. All findings appear equally important, delaying critical fixes while teams debate minor ones.

section.title: Agentic Workflow
section.p: Agent reads a feature spec or design document and generates a complete test plan (objectives, tasks, metrics, session script). After sessions are conducted by a human facilitator, the agent receives session notes and synthesizes findings: grouping observations by task, rating severity, drafting recommendations. Final pass produces executive summary and ranked action list.
section.reference: LLM prompt for generating a test plan from a feature spec
LLM prompt for synthesizing session notes into a prioritized findings report

antipattern.description: Agent generates task wording that includes solution hints ("click the settings icon").
antipattern.reason: Always review agent-generated task wording before use. LLMs default to instructional framing that invalidates tasks.

antipattern.description: Trusting agent severity ratings for Critical findings without human review.
antipattern.reason: Human-in-the-loop required for Critical finding classification before backlog entry. Stakes are too high for unreviewed AI judgment on blockers.
